transformation_type: Lewis-acid-mediated propargylic substitution/decomplexation of cobalt-alkyne complexes
mechanism_type: cobalt-stabilized propargylic cation formation followed by nucleophilic capture
common_conditions: formation of dicobalt hexacarbonyl complex then acid or Lewis-acid promoted decomplexation/substitution; oxidative or reductive decomplexation possible
description_short: Cobalt-complex-mediated propargylic substitution method that enables controlled generation and trapping of stabilized propargylic cations.

transformation_type: Ru-BINAP-catalyzed asymmetric hydrogenation of olefins and carbonyl compounds
mechanism_type: enantioselective hydrogen transfer/addition under Ru-BINAP catalysis
reactant_pattern_text: alpha,beta-unsaturated acid / beta-keto ester / functionalized ketone or alkene
common_conditions: hydrogenation under H2 pressure or transfer hydrogenation with chiral Ru-BINAP complexes gives high ee products
synonym_names: Noyori asymmetric hydrogenation|Noyori asymmetric transfer hydrogenation
description_short: Benchmark asymmetric hydrogenation platform using chiral BINAP-ruthenium catalysts to access enantioenriched alcohols, acids, and amines.

transformation_type: CrCl2-mediated carbonyl coupling with allyl, vinyl, aryl, or alkynyl halides
mechanism_type: organochromium formation followed by chemoselective carbonyl addition
common_conditions: organochromium reagent generated in situ from halides couples to aldehydes/ketones; Ni catalysis accelerates and broadens scope
description_short: Classic mild carbon-carbon bond-forming method that couples organohalides to carbonyl compounds through organochromium intermediates.
